rationale: An Administrator account executed a command to copy an executable (`WindowsServiceExample.exe`) to `C:\Program Files\windows_service.exe` and `C:\program.exe`. Subsequently, `sc.exe` was used to create (A0) and immediately start (EV-001) a new service named 'Example Service' configured to run the copied executable from `C:\Program Files\windows_service.exe` automatically. While service creation can be legitimate, the combination of copying an executable from an obfuscated path, creating a new service, and also copying the same executable to a generic name (`C:\program.exe`) without clear purpose, all executed by an Administrator, is highly suspicious and indicative of potential persistence or privilege escalation.
